evals.prompt:
/bibliotheque-librarian shelve --wiki klever

Shelve this nugget into the wiki. The content and source are in evals/files/nugget-dac-branch-model.md. Place it in the right section with full enrichment.
evals.expected_output: Classifies the nugget as an operational/deploy procedure, writes a new enriched page (frontmatter + 3-5 wikilinks + a How to apply line) into the correct section, and updates that section's INDEX.md. Shelve writes directly to a section, not to the inbox.
expectations: The agent classifies the nugget into an operational/procedure section (operations/ or sops/), not stack/
The agent writes a new page with YAML frontmatter including title, type, created, tags, and related
The page body contains 3-5 [[wikilinks]] to related wiki pages
The page includes a 'How to apply:' line for the procedure
The relevant section INDEX.md is updated with a one-line descriptive entry for the new page
The agent does NOT write the nugget to the inbox; shelve places directly into a section

evals.prompt:
/bibliotheque-librarian shelve --wiki klever

Shelve this debugging nugget. Content and source in evals/files/nugget-gateway-401.md. It is a failure symptom engineers will hit under pressure, so make sure it is reachable from the catalog.
evals.expected_output: Classifies the nugget into stack/ (service-to-service auth behavior) and, because it is a high-traffic crisis-time failure symptom, adds a Blocked-lane row to the root INDEX with a specific observable symptom. This root-lane row is the wiki's cross-cutting cross-reference surface.
expectations: The agent classifies the nugget into the stack/ section (service-to-service auth behavior)
The agent adds a row to the root INDEX.md Blocked lane ('I Am Blocked by an Error') because it is a crisis-time failure symptom
The Blocked-lane Symptom column is a specific observable failure naming the service pair and error (e.g. 'gateway -> subgraph returns 401 on dual auth headers'), not a generic term like '401 error'
The section INDEX.md is also updated with a one-liner for the new page
The page body carries 3-5 [[wikilinks]] and a 'How to apply:' line

evals.prompt:
/bibliotheque-librarian lint --wiki klever

Run the health scan. Note for context: the inbox has a pending entry at evals/files/phantom-inbox-entry.md whose nugget was already shelved directly into stack/gateway-dual-header-401.md, so treat that page as already existing on disk with the dual-header 401 content. The ledger still shows the entry pending.
evals.expected_output: In the phantom-pending reconcile check, greps verbatim key phrases from the pending entry against on-disk pages, confirms the content already exists, flips the entry Status to promoted (reconciled) and archives it, and creates no duplicate page.
expectations: The agent runs the phantom-pending reconcile check over the pending inbox entries
The agent greps 1-2 verbatim key phrases from the nugget to verify the content is already on disk, rather than trusting the ledger alone
On confirming the content exists, the agent flips the inbox entry Status to a reconciled/promoted state (e.g. 'promoted (reconciled by lint ...)')
The agent does NOT create a duplicate page or re-write the existing content
The reconciled entry is archived out of the hot inbox (moved to inbox/archive/YYYY/) rather than left in place

evals.prompt: /bibliotheque-librarian query "why does the Apollo gateway return a 401 to a subgraph?" --wiki klever
evals.expected_output: Searches the root INDEX / section INDEXes progressively, returns a synthesized answer citing the specific wiki page(s) it drew from, and writes nothing.
expectations: The agent searches the root INDEX and section INDEX files progressively (metadata before content), not by bulk-reading every wiki file
The answer includes citations: specific wiki file paths or page links it drew the answer from
The agent does NOT write, create, or modify any file in query mode
The answer is a synthesized response, not a raw dump of a file's full contents

evals.prompt:
/bibliotheque-librarian curate --wiki klever

Process the inbox. The pending entry to curate is evals/files/curate-inbox-entry.md (two nuggets, curator note included). Treat it as the single pending row in inbox/INDEX.md.
evals.expected_output: Reads the inbox INDEX and the entry, presents a placement plan for both nuggets before writing (plan-then-execute), then executes: routes each nugget to a section, updates section + root INDEX + ALIASES/LOG, marks the inbox entry promoted, and archives the raw file.
expectations: The agent reads the inbox INDEX and the pending entry file before writing anything
The agent presents a placement PLAN (target section, new-file vs extend, root-lane per nugget) BEFORE executing writes (plan-then-execute)
Each of the two nuggets is routed to a section consistent with the three-lane/section rules (stack/ or sops/)
After execution the inbox entry Status is marked promoted and the raw file is archived to inbox/archive/YYYY/ (Step 7b)
The agent updates the section INDEX and, where warranted, the root INDEX lanes and ALIASES/LOG
